note: Corpus says no drink cures cancer.

claim: The Eiffel Tower is about 330 meters tall.
Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize in Literature.
note: Corpus: she won Physics and Chemistry, never Literature.

claim: Adults need around 7 to 9 hours of sleep.
Supplements work better than therapy for anxiety.
note: Corpus: supplements are not a proven substitute for therapy.

claim: Human activity is the main cause of recent global warming.
note: Corpus states human activity is the dominant cause.

claim: Everyone must drink exactly eight glasses of water daily.
note: Corpus: this claim is not strongly supported; needs vary.

claim: Vitamin C prevents the common cold in everyone.
note: Corpus: does not prevent colds in the general population.

claim: Eating one carrot makes you invisible.
note: No relevant evidence exists in the corpus.

claim: The Eiffel Tower was painted blue in 1950.
note: Corpus has Eiffel facts but nothing about being painted blue.

claim: Bananas contain a hidden metal that powers phones.
note: No relevant evidence in the corpus.

claim: Investments can guarantee high returns with no risk.
note: Corpus: no investment guarantees returns; all carry risk.
